'''Richard David Myler''' (born 21 May 1990) is a retired [[rugby league]] footballer, who is currently the director of rugby for [[Hull FC]] in the Super League.<ref>{{cite web|title=Hull FC appoint retired Myler as director of rugby|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68819496|date=15 April 2024|publisher=BBC Sport}}</ref> He is a member of the [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland squad]] for the delayed [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]], having declared for Ireland in 2022. Previously he has represented [[England national rugby league team|England]] and the [[England Knights]] at international level. 

He has played for the [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], and the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]]  in the Championship and the [[Super League]]. Myler has also played for the [[Warrington Wolves]] and the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the Super League.

==Early life==
Myler was born in [[Widnes]], [[Cheshire, England]]. He attended St's Peter and Paul RC High school. He was amongst several students to receive a scholarship from the Rugby League.

==Playing career==
===Club career===
====Widnes Vikings====
Myler began his career with [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]], playing two games in the 2007 season.

====Salford: 2008–2009====
Myler signed for Salford from [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] on a three-year deal in 2008 and contributed to Salford winning the [[RFL Championship|National League One]] title, scoring 25 tries in 32 appearances,<ref>{{cite news|title=Myler Battle|url=http://www.thefreelibrary.com/MYLER+BATTLE.-a0198025197|newspaper=Sunday Mirror|date=19 April 2009|location=London|publisher=Trinity Mirror}}</ref>  and winning numerous awards as well as the Co-Operative Player of the Year award. On 14 February 2009 Myler made his [[Super League]] début against [[Crusaders Rugby League|Crusaders]], scoring a try in a Salford victory.

====Warrington: 2010–2015====
[[File:Richie Myler.JPG|thumb|right|Myler playing for Warrington in 2010]]
In 2009, he signed a four-year contract with [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] worth around £200,000, making him the most expensive teenage signing in Rugby League history.<ref>{{cite news
|url=http://www.thisischeshire.co.uk/sport/4585214.Smith_excited_to_work_with_new_boys/
|title=Smith excited to work with new boys
|newspaper=[[Warrington Guardian]]
|date=2009-09-07
|access-date=2011-08-21
|first=Connor|last=Murphy
}}</ref> Myler was offered a new contract which allegedly would have made him the highest paid player in the history of Widnes. According to agent [[Martin Offiah]] he rejected the deal, instead favouring a four-year deal with Warrington.<ref name="Richie Myler rejects Salford mega deal">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby/2009/06/23/myler-turns-down-reds-rags-to-richies-mega-deal-115875-21463953|title=Richie Myler rejects Salford mega deal|work=Daily Mirror|date=2009-06-23|access-date=2009-06-23}}</ref>{{better source needed|reason=tabloid|date=August 2025}}
[[File:Richard Myler.jpg|thumb|right|Myler playing for Warrington in 2012]]
He played in the [[2012 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/aug/25/leeds-warrington-challenge-cup |title=Warrington's battered Brett Hodgson recovers to see off Leeds in final|date=25 August 2012|work=Guardian UK|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref name="Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/oct/06/warrington-leeds-grand-final|title=Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington|work=The Guardian|date=6 October 2012|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19362692|title=Leeds Rhinos 18–35 Warrington Wolves|date=25 August 2012|work=BBC Sport|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web
 |url=http://www.warringtonwolves.org/warrington-wolves-are-challenge-cup-winners-2012-5628 
 |title=Warrington Wolves Are Challenge Cup Winners 2012! 
 |date=25 August 2012 
 |work=Warrington Wolves Official Site 
 |access-date=20 November 2019
 |url-status=dead 
 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131217230118/http://www.warringtonwolves.org/warrington-wolves-are-challenge-cup-winners-2012-5628 
 |archive-date=17 December 2013 
 |df=dmy 
}}</ref>

He played in the [[2012 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite news|title=Grand Final: Warrington 18-26 Leeds|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19805055|access-date=20 November 2019|work=BBC Sport|date=6 October 2012}}</ref><ref name="Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/oct/06/warrington-leeds-grand-final|title=Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington|work=The Guardian|date=6 October 2012|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref>

He played in the [[2013 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Wigan Warriors]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite news|last=Newsum|first=Matt|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan Warriors beat Warrington|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/24384930|access-date=13 November 2019|work=BBC Sport|date=5 October 2013}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|title=Super League Grand Final: Warrington v Wigan|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/rugby-league/24339698|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131007163603/http://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/rugby-league/24339698|url-status=dead|archive-date=7 October 2013|work=BBC Sport|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/10358228/Super-League-Grand-Final-2013-Warrington-Wolves-16-Wigan-Warriors-30-match-report.html|title= Warrington Wolves 16 Wigan Warriors 30|date=5 October 2013|work=Daily Telegraph|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2013/oct/05/warrington-wigan-super-league-grand-final|title= Wigan see off Warrington in X-rated Grand Final to complete double|date=5 October 2013|work=The Guardian |access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref>

====Catalans Dragons====
In 2015, he declared he was interested in playing the [[National Rugby League|NRL]] the next year as he wanted a new challenge. [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles|Manly]] were rumoured to want Myler after the departure of halves pairing [[Kieran Foran]] and [[Daly Cherry-Evans]].<ref>{{cite news 
|title = England halfback Richie Myler keen for NRL switch next season
|work = The Sydney Morning Herald
|date = 2015-04-12
|url = http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/sin-bin/england-halfback-richie-myler-keen-for-nrl-switch-next-season-20150410-1mivgd.html
|access-date = 2015-08-09}}</ref><ref>{{cite news 
|title = Manly Sea Eagles in market for Super League international halfback Richie Myler
|work = Fox Sports
|date = 2015-07-06
|url = http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-2015-manly-sea-eagles-in-market-for-super-league-international-halfback-richie-myler-exile-files/story-e6frf3ou-1227301442413
|access-date = 2015-08-09}}</ref> However, on 10 July 2015, it was confirmed that Myler would stay in the Super League, signing a two-year deal with [[Catalans Dragons]] from 2016.<ref>{{cite news 
|title = Warrington half-back Richie Myler to join Catalans Dragons in 2016
|publisher = Sky Sports
|date = 2015-07-10
|url = http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12216/9909320/warrington-halfback-richie-myler-to-join-catalans-dragons-in-2016
|access-date = 2015-07-27}}</ref>

====Leeds Rhinos====
On 27 June 2017 it was confirmed Myler had signed a three-year deal to join [[Leeds Rhinos]] starting from the following season.<ref name="Richie Myler: Leeds Rhinos sign half-back from Catalans Dragons">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/40418386|title=Richie Myler: Leeds Rhinos sign half-back from Catalans Dragons|publisher=bbc.co.uk|date=27 June 2017|access-date=1 January 2018}}</ref>  Myler was named [[Lance Todd Trophy]] winner for his performance at {{rlp|FB}} in the [[2020 Challenge Cup Final]].<ref>{{cite news |title=Leeds' Luke Gale's drop goal steal's Challenge Cup from Salford |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2020/oct/17/leeds-salford-rugby-league-challenge-cup-match-report |access-date=17 October 2020 |work=The Guardian |date=17 October 2020 |language=en}}</ref>
In round 21 of the 2022 Super League season, Myler scored a [[hat-trick]] in Leeds 36–32 victory over the [[Catalans Dragons]].  Leeds had been down 32–6 in the second half with one player being issued with a red card.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/62321042|title=Super League: Catalans Dragons 32-36 Leeds Rhinos - Visitors win in golden point extra time|work=BBC Sport}}</ref>
In round 25 of the 2022 Super League season, Leeds defeated [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] 18–14 in controversial circumstances. With less than five minutes to play, Myler was caught in a tackle with Huddersfield's Chris Hill.The referee issued a yellow card to Hill over the incident which moments later eventuated in Leeds scoring the winning try.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.leeds-live.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/phil-clarke-richie-myler-leeds-24847815/|title='It's a disgrace' - Phil Clarke's fury after controversial Richie Myler incident|website=www.leeds-live.co.uk}}</ref>
On 24 September 2022, Myler played for Leeds in their 24–12 loss to [[St Helens RFC]] in the [[2022 Super League Grand Final]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2022/sep/24/st-helens-leeds-super-league-grand-final-match-report-rugby-league/|title=St Helens sink Leeds to win record fourth consecutive Grand Final|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>
Myler played a total of 19 games for Leeds in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished 8th on the table and missed the playoffs.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leeds-rhinos-2023-season-review-picking-out-the-best-and-worst-players-performances-results-4355920/|title=Leeds Rhinos' 2023 season review: picking out the best and worst players, performances, results|website=www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk}}</ref>

===York Knights===
On 6 November 2023, it was reported that he had signed for [[York Knights]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a one-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=6 Nov 2023|title=Leeds Rhinos stalwart Richie Myler departs in surprise Championship move: ‘The time felt right for a new challenge’|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/leeds-rhinos-stalwart-richie-myler-departs-in-surprise-championship-move-the-time-felt-right-for-a-new-challenge}}</ref> On 3 March 2024, Myler made his 400th career appearance in York's [[RFL 1895 Cup|1895 Cup]] quarter-final match against Oldham.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/62517/ab-sundecks-1895-cup-review-%7C-quarter-finals |title=AB Sundecks 1895 Cup Review {{!}} Quarter Finals |publisher=RFL |date=4 March 2024 |access-date=15 April 2024}}</ref> In April, Myler announced his retirement following an elbow injury sustained in a match against Doncaster the previous month.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkrlfc.com/post/club-statement-richie-myler |title=Club Statement: Richie Myler |publisher=York RLFC |date=15 April 2024 |access-date=15 April 2024}}</ref>

===International career===
====England====
Myler has represented both the [[Lancashire]] and [[England national rugby league team|England]] U-17s at Academy Level.

He was named in the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#England|England training squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Myler gets England call">{{cite web|url=http://www.englandrl.co.uk/article.php?id=11890|title=Myler gets England call|publisher=England Rugby League|date=2008-09-29|access-date=2008-10-03|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20081007232803/http://www.englandrl.co.uk/article.php?id=11890|archive-date=2008-10-07}}</ref> He was named in the [[England national rugby league team|England]] team to face [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] at the [[Keepmoat Stadium]], [[Doncaster]] prior to England's departure for the 2008 Rugby League World Cup.<ref name="Gleeson to lead new-look England">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7642490.stm|title=Gleeson to lead new-look England|publisher=BBC|date=2008-10-09|access-date=2008-10-10}}</ref> He made his England début in the victory over [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] on 10 October 2008.<ref name="England warm up in style">{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12196_4297504,00.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20081013034008/http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12196_4297504,00.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=2008-10-13|title=England warm up in style|work=Sky Sports|date=2008-10-10|access-date=2008-10-11}}</ref>

Myler represented England against [[France national rugby league team|France]] on 13 June 2009. He started the game as a {{rlp|sh}} and scored 30 points in the 66–12 England victory.<ref name="France 12-66 England:Richie Myler stars as Lions romp to victory">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby/2009/06/14/smiler-myler-115875-21438919|title=France 12-66 England:Richie Myler stars as Lions romp to victory|work=Daily Mirror|date=2009-06-14|access-date=2009-06-23}}</ref>

Myler was not called up to the England RL side for the Four Nations in 2011, however he was selected in the England Knights where he put in two man of the match performances but was still not called up.{{citation needed|date=August 2025}}

Myler was recalled to the international stage in 2018, playing in test matches against [[France national rugby league team|France]] at the [[Leigh Sports Village]]<ref name="Tom Johnstone marks debut with England hat-trick against France">{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2018/10/17/tom-johnstone-marks-debut-england-hat-trick-against-france/|title=Tom Johnstone marks debut with England hat-trick against France|work=The Daily Telegraph|date=17 October 2018|access-date=31 October 2019}}</ref> and New Zealand.

====Ireland====
In August 2022 Myler declared that he was available to play for {{rlnt|Ireland}} in the forthcoming [[2021 Rugby League World Cup|World Cup]]. He is qualified to play for Ireland through one of his grandfathers.<ref>{{cite web |title=Richie Myler to honour Irish grandad's wish at Rugby League World Cup |url=https://www.intrl.sport/news/i-d-love-to-see-you-in-green-myler-to-honour-irish-grandad-s-wish/ |website=Rugby League International Federation |access-date=30 September 2022 |date=17 August 2022}}</ref> Myler was named in the Ireland squad for the tournament when it was announced on 29 September 2022.<ref>{{cite news |title=Keary and Myler in Ireland World Cup squad |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/63074611 |access-date=30 September 2022 |work=BBC Sport |date=29 September 2022}}</ref>

==Personal life==
In March 2012 Myler became engaged to then ''[[Blue Peter]]'' presenter [[Helen Skelton]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/3am/celebrity-news/sport-relief-star-helen-skeltons-759452|title= "I am a very lucky lady!": Helen Skelton's delight at getting engaged|publisher= Daily Mirror|date=2012-03-13|first=Steve|last=White|access-date=2012-03-17}}</ref> They married in December 2013,<ref>{{cite news|last=Watts|first=Halina|title=BT Sport beauty Helen Skelton ties the knot with rugby league star Richie Myler|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/tv/tv-news/bt-sport-beauty-helen-skelton-2953900|access-date=2 January 2014|newspaper=Mirror TV|date=22 December 2013}}</ref> and Skelton gave birth to their first child, a boy named Ernie, on 19 June 2015.<ref name="Former Blue Peter presenter Helen Skelton welcomes baby boy">{{cite web|url=http://www.digitalspy.co.uk/showbiz/news/a654124/former-blue-peter-presenter-helen-skelton-welcomes-baby-boy.html|title=Former Blue Peter presenter Helen Skelton welcomes baby boy|date=21 June 2015|work=Digital Spy|access-date=22 June 2015}}</ref> followed by their second son, Louis, in April 2017. The couple's daughter, Elsie Kate, was born on 28 December 2021. Skelton announced on 25 April 2022 that the couple had separated.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Roberts |first1=Jamie |last2=Brock |first2=Alexander |title=Countryfile presenter Helen Skelton announces split from husband |url=https://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/news/showbiz-news/countryfile-presenter-helen-skelton-announces-23785638 |access-date=1 May 2022 |work=Manchester Evening News |date=26 April 2022}}</ref>

It was later revealed on 6 March 2023 on Instagram that his new girlfriend, heiress, Stephanie Thirkill, was pregnant with their first child.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Shaw |first=Megan |date=2022-11-07 |title=Stephanie Thirkill 'proud' as she makes special request to Richie Myler |url=https://www.leeds-live.co.uk/news/celebs-tv/stephanie-thirkill-proud-makes-special-25457062 |access-date=2022-11-13 |website=LeedsLive |language=en}}</ref> The child was born in April 2023.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Shaw |first=Megan |date=2023-05-15 |title=Helen Skelton’s ex Richie Myler shares first picture of new baby as adorable name is revealed|url=https://www.leeds-live.co.uk/news/celebs-tv/helen-skeltons-ex-richie-myler-26918383 |access-date=2023-10-14 |website=LeedsLive |language=en}}</ref>
